zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser於1286年出生在英國武斯特斯爾亨利 卡素時，他的父親Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester25歲，母親Lady Isabella de Beauchamp38歲。他於1306年6月14日與Eleanor de Clare在英國英格蘭倫敦維斯特明斯特結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1326年11月24日在英國賀福爾賀福德享年40歲，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利福可斯波利 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser was born in 1286, in Hanley Castle, Worcestershire, England, his father, Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester, was 25 and his mother, Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, was 38. He married Eleanor de Clare on 14 June 1306, in Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 24 November 1326, in Hereford, Herefordshire, England, at the age of 40, and was buried in Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser est né en 1286, à Hanley Castle, Worcestershire, Angleterre, son père, Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Eleanor de Clare le 14 juin 1306, à Westminster, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 24 novembre 1326, à Hereford, Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterré à Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser 1286 in Hanley Castle, Worcestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester 25 und seine Mutter Lady Isabella de Beauchamp 38. Er hat am 14. Juni 1306 in Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom Eleanor de Clare geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Er starb am 24. November 1326 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Hereford, Herefordshire, England und wurde in Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser è nato il nel 1286 nel Hanley Castle, Worcestershire, England, il suo padre, Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, aveva 38 anni. Lui sposato Eleanor de Clare il 14 giugno 1306, nel Westminster, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 24 novembre 1326, nel Hereford, Herefordshire, England, all'età di 40 anni, e fu sepolto a Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenserが1286年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアハンリー カストルで生まれた時，父Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchesterは25歳，母Lady Isabella de Beauchampは38歳であった。1306年6月14日にイギリスイギリスロンドンウエストミンスターでEleanor de Clareと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1326年11月24日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアハーエフォードで40歳で死去し，イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアテウケスブリーテウケスブリー アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser(이)가 1286년 영국 워스스터실르 핸리 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Isabella de Beauchamp(은)는 38세였다. 그는 1306년 6월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터에서 Eleanor de Clare(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1326년 11월 24일 영국 허퍼드실르 허퍼드에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르 튜케스버이 튜케스버이 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser nasceu em 1286, em Hanley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, tinha 38 anos. Ele casou-se com Eleanor de Clare em 14 de junho de 1306, em Westminster, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 24 de novembro de 1326, em Hereford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, com 40 anos, e foi sepultado em Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser родился в 1286 г. в местности ханли кастл, ворсестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor de Clare 14 июня 1306 г. в местности вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 7 дочерей. Он умер 24 ноября 1326 г. в местности хирефорд, хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 40 лет и был похоронен в местности тюкесбури эбби, тюкесбури, глоусестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser nació en 1286, en Hanley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, tenía 38 años. Se casó con Eleanor de Clare el 14 de junio de 1306, en Westminster, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 24 de noviembre de 1326, en Hereford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 40 años, y fue sepultado en Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.
